–Symbolical uses of
–BLACK: A SYMBOL OF AFFLICTION AND CALAMITYJob 3:5;10:20-22;30:26;Ps 107:10,11;143:3;Isa 5:30;8:22;9:19;24:11;50:3;Joe 2:6,10;3:14,15;Am 5:8;Na 2:10;Zep 1:14,15;Mt 8:12;22:13;25:30;2Pe 2:4;Jude 1:13;Re 16:10
–BLUE: SYMBOL OF DEITYEx 24:10;25:3,4;26:1;28:28,37;38:18;39:1-5,21,24,29,31;Nu 4:5-12;15:38-40;2Ch 2:7,14;3:14;Jer 10:9;Eze 
1:26;10:1
  .For additional reference, see passages below under CRIMSON
  .SYMBOL OF ROYALTYEs 8:15;Eze 23:6
  .See CRIMSON, below
–CRIMSON, RED, PURPLE, AND SCARLET, SYMBOLS OF VARIOUS IDEAS
  .Of iniquityIsa 1:18;Re 17:3,4;18:12,16
  .Of royaltyJud 8:26;Da 5:7,16,29;Mt 27:28
  .Prosperity2Sa 1:24;Pr 31:21;La 4:5
  .ConquestNa 2:3;Re 12:3
  .These colors figured largely in the symbolisms of the tabernacle 
furnishing, and priestly vestments and functions, as types and shadows of the atonementEx 25:3-5;26:1,14,31,36;36:8,19,35,37;27:16;28:4-8,15,31,33;35:5-7,23,25,35;38:18,23;39:2,3,5,29;Le 14:4,6,49,51,52;Nu 4:7,8,13;19:2,5,6;Isa 63:1-3;
